name: Bulls Eye Cheesecake (Kh)
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust rack 1/3 up from the bottom of the oven and preheat to 350°F.
2. Carefully butter an 8-inch cheesecake pan all the way up to the rim, including the inside rim itself. The cake will stick to the rim as it rises and will not rise evenly if left unstuck.
3. Place a larger pan (for hot water) in preparation; ensure it is not deeper than the cheesecake pan but wide enough so it does not touch the sides of the cake pan.
4. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese until soft and smooth, scraping sides and beaters frequently with a rubber spatula to ensure uniform smoothness.
5. Beat in sour cream, vanilla, almond extract, salt, and eggs one at a time, scraping the bowl occasionally and beating after each addition until incorporated.
6. Remove the mixture into another bowl; you will have 6 cups total.
7. Divide the mixture: add half (3 cups) to one bowl for sugar and the other half to another bowl for brown sugar.
8. Stir sugar ingredients in one bowl until dissolved, and mixtures in the other bowl until thinned out.
9. To the dark mixture, stir instant coffee and cocoa powder through a fine strainer until dissolved and no specks are visible.
10. Alternate placing 1 cup of each mixture in the prepared pan; pour directly into the middle, spreading to cover the bottom.
11. Repeat with the second color, continuing until all batters are used or four additions of each mixture are complete.
12. Carefully handle the pan and place it in the larger pan filled with hot water about 1-1/2 inches deep. If using an aluminum pan, add about 1 tsp cream of tartar to prevent discoloration.
13. Transfer to the oven and bake for 1-1/2 hours.
14. Remove the cake pan from the hot water and let it cool completely until the bottom reaches room temperature.
15. Unmold by dipping the bottom of the pan in boiling water for 10-15 seconds, then drying the pan before turning it over with a plate or board to unmold the cake.
16. Refrigerate; serve slices best when dipped into very hot water before cutting each piece. The hotter the water, the better.
